sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Korea Electric Power Corporation (KEPCO), a major shareholder and contractor, will push for the firs..
feed.summary: Korea Electric Power Corporation (KEPCO) plans to secure its first dividend from the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant in the UAE in the first half of this year. This move is expected to help improve KEPCO's financial structure, which currently faces a cumulative deficit of 23.1 trillion won, and potentially lead to additional orders for UAE nuclear power plants. KEPCO holds an 18% stake in the operating joint venture, Nawah, and the dividend will mark the first time it secures profits from equity investments rather than just construction costs.

feed.title: FERMI CLASS ACTION REMINDER: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C.
feed.summary: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. has filed a class action lawsuit against Fermi, Inc. (NASDAQ: FRMI) on behalf of investors who purchased shares related to its October 2025 IPO or between October 1, 2025, and December 11, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that Fermi made false statements regarding its "Project Matador" campus, including overstating tenant demand and potential reliance on a single tenant's funding commitment. Investors suffering losses are encouraged to contact the firm before the March 6, 2026, deadline to apply for lead plaintiff.

feed.title: Nio to ramp up bet on AI in 2026, William Li says at internal meeting [Full Speech]
feed.summary: Nio plans to significantly increase its investment in Artificial Intelligence (AI) in 2026, with CEO William Li urging employees to embrace AI for improved product competitiveness and operational efficiency across all business segments. Li, who views automotive companies with ambition as inherently AI companies, also expressed confidence in achieving Nio's first-ever quarterly profitability in Q4 2025 and projected over RMB 10 billion in non-automotive revenue for 2026. The company aims for 40-50% annual growth and improved vehicle gross margins, building on overcoming challenges in the previous year.

feed.title: FTAI Aviation (NASDAQ:FTAI) Trading 8.8% Higher - What's Next?
feed.summary: FTAI Aviation (NASDAQ:FTAI) shares increased by 8.8% to $274.33 during mid-day trading on Thursday. The company reported its last earnings on Monday, October 27th, with an EPS of $1.10, missing analyst estimates. Several analysts have updated their ratings on FTAI, with a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and a target price of $226.88.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley beats consensus, defends keeping older goals
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley reported strong fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results, exceeding analyst expectations driven by a surge in investment banking revenues. Despite this performance and a request from an analyst to raise targets, CEO Ted Pick defended the bank's decision to maintain its existing financial goals, emphasizing prudence and consistent earnings compoundment through economic cycles. The bank got closer to its $10 trillion client asset target and already surpassed its efficiency ratio and return on tangible common equity goals.

feed.title: Verizon users report service outage affecting thousands
feed.summary: Verizon users are reporting a widespread service outage that is affecting thousands of customers. The nature or cause of the outage was not specified in the provided content.

feed.title: PNC renews lease at namesake Downtown Louisville tower
feed.summary: PNC Bank has renewed its lease for another ten years at its namesake tower in Downtown Louisville. This decision reflects the city's largest bank's commitment to Downtown Louisville, with plans for office upgrades as more employees return to in-person work. The renewal comes as PNC also mandates a full return to the office for its employees.

feed.title: Chinese device maker moves toward Nasdaq listing after SEC green light
feed.summary: ExcelFin Acquisition Corp (NASDAQ: XFIN) announced that the U.S. SEC has declared effective the Form F-4 registration statement for its proposed business combination with Baird Medical Investment Holdings Limited. This is a crucial step towards Baird Medical becoming a Nasdaq-listed public company. ExcelFin has scheduled a Special Meeting of stockholders for September 26, 2024, to vote on the transaction, which, if approved, is expected to close shortly thereafter.

feed.title: Barclays Forecasts Strong Price Appreciation for Expeditors International of Washington (NASDAQ:EXPD) Stock
feed.summary: Barclays has increased its price target for Expeditors International of Washington (NASDAQ:EXPD) from $110 to $135, though it maintained an "underweight" rating, suggesting a potential 17.8% downside from its previous close. Despite this, other analysts have varied views, with the consensus being a "Hold" and an average target of $134.10, while some firms have even upgraded EXPD to a "Strong Buy." The stock is currently trading near its 52-week high, with a market cap of $22.01 billion and a P/E ratio of 28.7.

feed.title: FAT Brands Faces Nasdaq Delisting, Bankruptcy With Massive Debt and Low Stock Price
feed.summary: FAT Brands is on the verge of being delisted from Nasdaq due to its stock price falling below $1, compounding issues from $1.3 billion in securitized debt that was declared "immediately due." The company is exploring bankruptcy and struggling to renegotiate with 25 diverse investors across five different securitization trusts. This financial distress follows a series of acquisitions and recent controversies involving CEO Andy Wiederhorn, with same-store sales suffering and key executives receiving significant salary increases amid bonus waivers.

feed.title: Coterra Energy exploring potential merger deal with Devon Energy - Bloomberg (CTRA:NYSE)
feed.summary: Coterra Energy is reportedly in talks with Devon Energy regarding a potential merger. This deal, if it materializes, would be one of the largest in the oil and gas sector in recent years. Following the Bloomberg report, Coterra Energy's stock saw a 3.2% increase in trading.

feed.title: Raymond James Financial to Acquire $46 Billion Asset Manager
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial has announced its agreement to acquire Clark Capital Management, a Philadelphia-based asset manager with $46 billion in investments. This acquisition aims to expand Raymond James’ multiunit asset-management strategy. The deal is anticipated to be finalized in the third quarter.

feed.title: Barclays Maintains Overweight Rating on Duke Energy (DUK) but Lo
feed.summary: Barclays has reiterated its "Overweight" rating on Duke Energy (DUK) but reduced its price target from $135.00 to $127.00. This adjustment follows similar moves by other firms like UBS and JP Morgan, though Mizuho recently increased its price target. Duke Energy is a major US utility, and the average one-year price target from 18 analysts suggests a 12.22% upside from its current price, with a consensus "Outperform" recommendation.

feed.title: Barclays Revises CenterPoint Energy (CNP) Price Target to $38
feed.summary: Barclays has maintained its Equal-Weight rating for CenterPoint Energy (CNP) but lowered its price target from $41.00 to $38.00, a 7.32% decrease. This adjustment was made by analyst Nicholas Campanella on January 15, 2026. Despite the revised target from Barclays, the average target price from 15 analysts is $42.18, suggesting a potential upside, while GuruFocus estimates a downside based on its GF Value.

feed.title: Barclays Adjusts Price Target for Atmos Energy (ATO) to $165 | A
feed.summary: Barclays has lowered its price target for Atmos Energy (ATO) from $172.00 to $165.00, while maintaining an "Equal-Weight" rating. This adjustment reflects a 4.07% reduction from the previous target. Despite varied analyst ratings, the average one-year target price from 11 analysts is $175.74, suggesting a 3.80% upside from the current price.

feed.title: GlobalFoundries acquires ARC and RISC-V IP from Synopsys — company gains critical CPU IP as it grows beyond being a mere chipmaker
feed.summary: GlobalFoundries has acquired the Processor IP Solutions business, including ARC and RISC-V IP, from Synopsys. This move, following the MIPS acquisition, significantly expands GlobalFoundries' CPU IP portfolio, aiming to broaden its offerings for automotive, embedded, industrial, and wearable applications. The acquisition allows GlobalFoundries to provide a more integrated path from architecture to silicon, solidifying its role beyond that of a traditional chipmaker.

feed.title: Coterra, Devon in talks over potential Permian mega-merger
feed.summary: Coterra Energy Inc. and Devon Energy Corp. are reportedly in discussions regarding a potential all-stock merger, which could result in one of the largest oil and gas deals in years. Both companies hold significant assets in the Permian Basin, with market capitalizations of around $21 billion and $23 billion respectively. While negotiations are ongoing, there is no guarantee an agreement will be reached, and Coterra has also engaged in merger talks with other firms previously.

feed.title: Trade Tracker: Bill Baruch sells T-Mobile and buys more Cheniere, SLB and ExxonMobil
feed.summary: Bill Baruch, Founder & President of Blue Line Capital, discussed his recent portfolio adjustments on CNBC’s “Halftime Report.” He indicated that he sold T-Mobile stock and increased his positions in energy companies including Cheniere, SLB, and ExxonMobil. The segment also touched on the Committee's broader energy strategy.

feed.title: 3M heads into earnings exemplifying market shift to value, with plenty of room to rally
feed.summary: 3M (MMM) is highlighted as a Dow stock poised for a rally, reflecting a market shift towards value. The article notes its 23.5% gain over the last 52 weeks and identifies a key resistance level at $172, with fourth-quarter earnings acting as a potential catalyst for a breakout. Technical analysis suggests further upside, with short-term targets around $190 and long-term potential to return to 2021 levels or even all-time highs.

feed.title: Halper Sadeh Investigates Cummins Inc. Officers for Breach of Fiduciary Duties
feed.summary: Halper Sadeh LLC is investigating Cummins Inc. officers for potential breaches of fiduciary duties, aiming for governance reforms and shareholder value enhancement. Long-term shareholders may seek legal relief, with their participation being crucial for transparency and accountability. The article also provides analyst ratings and price targets for CMI, noting a general expectation of a stock price fall despite some recent upgrades.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley to Sell $8 Billion From US Dollar Bond Sale
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley is selling $8 billion in U.S. dollar bonds, exceeding its initial target of approximately $6.5 billion. This sale comes as Wall Street banks are actively entering the debt markets following their earnings reports. The offering includes notes with maturities ranging from four to fifteen years, with one segment tightening significantly in yield spread.

feed.title: Lam Research Corp. (LRCX) Receives Bullish Upgrades with Price Targets Up to $260
feed.summary: Lam Research (LRCX) has received bullish analyst upgrades, with RBC Capital initiating coverage with an Outperform rating and a $260 price target, and Wells Fargo raising its target to $250. The company anticipates strong earnings for its January 28, 2026 conference call, with projected year-over-year increases in EPS and revenue. LRCX shares have shown robust performance, trading significantly above its 20-day simple moving average and increasing by 186.99% over the past year.

feed.title: Kenvue stock is up today — here’s what the Kimberly-Clark deal is worth now
feed.summary: Kenvue shares increased slightly following a rise in Kimberly-Clark stock, impacting the implied value of their pending takeover deal. Investors are closely monitoring the Jan. 29 shareholder meeting and potential litigation headlines, as the value of the cash-and-stock transaction fluctuates with Kimberly-Clark's share price. The deal, which includes $3.50 cash plus 0.14625 Kimberly-Clark shares per Kenvue share, is currently valued at about $18.10 per Kenvue share and is expected to close in the second half of 2026, pending approvals.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Wealth Division Posts Record Revenue Growth
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley's wealth division achieved record revenue growth, with net revenues up 13% year-over-year and total client assets reaching $9.3 trillion. CEO Ted Pick acknowledged the "complicated" geopolitical backdrop but maintained the firm's long-term financial goals despite accelerated performance. This growth mirrors strong wealth management results reported by other major wirehouses like Bank of America Merrill Lynch, Wells Fargo, and Goldman Sachs.

feed.title: Warren Buffett to Step Down as Berkshire CEO by End of 2025, Company Holds Nearly 3 Million Shares of Domino's Pizza
feed.summary: This article reports on Warren Buffett's announcement to step down as Berkshire CEO by the end of 2025 and highlights Berkshire Hathaway's significant holding of nearly 3 million shares in Domino's Pizza. It details Domino's strong sales growth, profitability improvement, and expansion potential, along with robust free cash flow. The piece also includes analyst views and updated price targets for DPZ stock.

feed.title: Report: Boston-Based TradFi Giant State Street to Roll out Tokenized Products
feed.summary: State Street, a major traditional financial institution based in Boston, is reportedly preparing to launch tokenized products. This move signifies a significant step for a "TradFi" giant into the world of digital assets, leveraging blockchain technology for financial products. The initiative is expected to cater to institutional clients, bringing established financial players further into the crypto and blockchain space.

feed.title: Dueling slides and bigger pool deck: NCL reveals largest cruise ship
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line announced its largest ship to date, Norwegian Aura, set to launch in 2027. The vessel will be 10% larger than previous ships and feature a new open-air activity complex called Ocean Heights with five waterslides and a larger pool deck. Aura will debut in the Mediterranean before homeporting in Miami for Caribbean sailings, with cruises starting at $1,263 per person.

feed.title: BXP Completes More Than $1 Billion in Strategic Property Sales
feed.summary: BXP (NYSE: BXP) has successfully completed over $1 billion in strategic property dispositions, moving significantly closer to its multi-year asset sales target of $1.9 billion between 2025 and 2027. These transactions include the sale of suburban land parcels, residential properties, and non-core office and life sciences properties across various regions. The initiative aims to raise capital for premier workplace developments and increase BXP's concentration in central business districts within core gateway markets.

feed.title: Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte (NASDAQ:OMAB) Sees Unusually-High Trading Volume - Still a Buy?
feed.summary: Shares of Grupo Aeroportuario del Centro Norte (NASDAQ:OMAB) experienced an unusually high trading volume, with 75,831 shares changing hands, a 65% increase from the previous session. The stock closed at $105.60, down from $107.93. Analyst ratings are mixed, with some upgrades to "Strong Buy" while the MarketBeat consensus remains "Hold" with an average target price of $102.50, and the company recently paid a dividend of $2.5219 per share.

feed.title: Capital City Bank Group (NASDAQ:CCBG) Sees Strong Trading Volume - Here's What Happened
feed.summary: Capital City Bank Group (NASDAQ:CCBG) experienced a slight decrease in trading volume but continues to show strong underlying fundamentals and positive analyst sentiment. The bank reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates, and maintains a "Buy" rating with an average target price of $45.00. Institutional investors have actively adjusted their positions, indicating ongoing interest in the company.

feed.title: Trade Tracker: Malcolm Ethridge buys JPMorgan and Morgan Stanley
feed.summary: Malcolm Ethridge, Managing Partner at Capital Area Planning Group, disclosed his recent investments in JPMorgan and Morgan Stanley on CNBC's Halftime Report. The segment focused on Ethridge detailing his latest purchases in these two major financial institutions. The Committee further discussed the broader financial sector.

feed.title: Becton, Dickinson Wins FDA Clearance For Next-Gen Breast Biopsy Device, Launch Set For 2026
feed.summary: Becton, Dickinson and Company (NYSE: BDX) received 510(k) clearance from the FDA for its new EnCor EnCompass Breast Biopsy and Tissue Removal System, which is slated for an early 2026 market launch. This device aims to enhance breast biopsy procedures across various imaging modalities with advanced features. While the company's stock shows bullish momentum alongside broader market gains, Benzinga Edge notes a "High-Flyer" setup, cautioning investors about its premium valuation.

feed.title: Genesco to Overhaul Tech and Accelerate AI-enabled Innovations, Expects Job Cuts
feed.summary: Genesco, parent company of Journeys, is set to transform its information technology operating model by partnering with a global technology provider and leveraging AI-enabled innovations to support long-term growth and improve speed and flexibility. This overhaul, expected to be implemented over 12-18 months, will unfortunately result in job cuts within its IT organization, though the exact number is yet to be determined. The news follows a strong holiday sales performance for Genesco, with comparable sales increasing by 9 percent for the fourth quarter-to-date period ended December 27.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Group Launches Points Choice to Enhance Loyalty Across Brands
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group is launching "Points Choice," a new feature allowing loyalty program members to earn and redeem points across its three brands: Royal Caribbean, Celebrity Cruises, and Silversea. This initiative, building on the 2024 Status Match program, offers greater flexibility for guests to direct their loyalty points to the program where they want to build status. The Points Choice system goes into effect on January 30, 2026, for sailings departing on or after that date.

feed.title: Bruno Mars' 'The Romantic Tour' breaks LiveNation record, prompts second Detroit show
feed.summary: Bruno Mars' "The Romantic Tour" has broken LiveNation's single-day ticket sales record for North America, leading to a second show being added in Detroit due to overwhelming demand. Mars will play at Ford Field on May 9-10, joined by Anderson .Paak as DJ Pee .Wee and Leon Thomas. This tour marks Mars' first global stadium outing in nearly a decade and precedes the release of his fourth solo album, "The Romantic," on February 27.

feed.title: Fortive Corp. Stock Is Quietly Popping Off: Smart Money Move Or Overhyped Snoozefest?
feed.summary: This article examines Fortive Corp. (FTV) stock, posing the question of whether it's a smart investment or overhyped. It highlights Fortive's transition from a traditional industrial company to one focused on high-margin software, sensors, and data-driven tools, leading to recurring revenue and increased profitability potential. The piece suggests that while FTV isn't a viral sensation, it garners quiet respect among finance creators and long-term investors looking for steady compounders in their portfolios.

feed.title: American Express Reports Stable Card Lending and Credit Metrics
feed.summary: American Express reported stable card lending and credit metrics for the period ending December 31, 2025, with modest growth in U.S. consumer and small business card loans. Delinquency and write-off rates remained stable, and the American Express Credit Account Master Trust showed solid credit performance. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates AXP as "Outperform" due to its strong financial performance, robust revenue growth, and strategic focus on premium products.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Raises Price Target for ULTA to $750, Maintains O
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has raised its price target for Ulta Beauty (ULTA) to $750 from $640, while maintaining an Overweight rating. This analysis, led by Simeon Gutman, reflects a positive outlook despite the average target price from 23 analysts suggesting a slight downside from the current stock price. Ulta Beauty is the largest specialized beauty retailer in the US, with operations expanding internationally, and analysts generally rate it as "Outperform."

feed.title: TD Cowen Lowers Price Target for Bank of America (BAC) to $64
feed.summary: TD Cowen has lowered its price target for Bank of America (BAC) from $66 to $64 but maintained a "Buy" rating. This adjustment follows various analyst ratings over recent weeks, showcasing a spectrum of opinions on BAC's potential performance. Despite the lowered target, the average analyst price target for BAC sits at $60.69, suggesting an upside from its current price, while GuruFocus estimates a potential downside based on its fair value calculations.

feed.title: Will Futu’s (FUTU) Rapid Revenue Growth and 40% Margins Reshape Its Risk‑Reward Narrative?
feed.summary: Futu Holdings Limited (FUTU) has demonstrated rapid revenue growth and maintained high net margins around 40% between 2021 and 2024, driven by its Futubull and moomoo platforms. This strong financial performance, including Q3 2025 revenue of HK$6,402.9 million and net income of HK$3,227.7 million, suggests operating leverage and user growth are effectively translating into earnings. While supporting its investment narrative focused on scaling a digital brokerage model and investing in AI-driven upgrades, the company faces ongoing risks from tighter regulatory scrutiny and market volatility.

feed.title: authID Inc. Stock (AUID) Opinions on ServiceNow Integration
feed.summary: AuthID Inc. (AUID) experienced a significant stock surge, reportedly nearly 94% in one day, driven by its new integration with ServiceNow for biometric identity verification in contact centers. This development highlights the potential for seamless, spoof-resistant logins and enterprise adoption, especially given AuthID's focus on deepfake-resistant technology. The article also provides details on recent insider trading activity, quarterly revenue for AUID, and institutional investor movements.

feed.title: What is International Paper's 2026 Manufacturing Strategy?
feed.summary: International Paper (IP) is transitioning to become a global leader in sustainable packaging, focusing on innovation, circularity, and the acquisition of DS Smith. This strategy involves divesting from commodity pulp products, strengthening its North American market presence through facility repurposing and closures, and enhancing client satisfaction with high-quality, on-time delivery. Despite market challenges and revised box shipment projections, IP aims for significant EBITDA growth by 2027 by emphasizing its customer-centric approach and sustainable offerings.

feed.title: South Korean defense ETFs surge as geopolitical tensions mount
feed.summary: South Korean defense-related ETFs have significantly outperformed other sectors, including semiconductors, this year due to escalating geopolitical tensions globally. Leveraged defense ETFs saw gains exceeding 60%, while non-leveraged ones rose around 30%. This surge is attracting overseas capital and driving up stock prices for major South Korean defense firms like Hanwha Systems, as the government aims to become a top global defense exporter.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley see double-digit profit jumps amid surging stock market
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley both reported significant profit increases in the fourth quarter, driven by a surging stock market and a rise in deal-making. Goldman Sachs saw a 12% profit jump, while Morgan Stanley's profit rose to $4.4 billion. Both banks benefited from strong investment fee revenues and increased investment fee backlogs, even as other large banks faced tensions with the White House over Federal Reserve independence and credit card interest rates.

feed.title: Loop Industries (LOOP) Secures Long-Term Contract with Nike as India Facility Construction Progresses on Schedule
feed.summary: Loop Industries has secured a long-term contract with Nike for a fixed annual supply of polyester resin, including a take-or-pay clause, which is expected to stabilize revenue. The company's Infinite Loop India facility is on track for completion by late 2027, with management optimistic about financing. Loop also reported reduced operating expenses in Q3 and foresees growing demand for its recycling technology due to European regulations, boosting its textile and packaging markets.

feed.title: National Vision Holdings stock hits 52-week high at $29.90
feed.summary: National Vision Holdings Inc. (EYE) stock reached a new 52-week high of $29.90, currently trading slightly higher at $29.91. This represents a significant 163.49% increase over the past year, reflecting strong investor confidence and operational efficiency with gross profit margins of 58.69%. The company reported strong Q3 2025 financial results, exceeding revenue forecasts and raising full-year revenue guidance, with analysts maintaining an optimistic "Buy" rating and a high price target of $39.

feed.title: Wells Fargo CEO Touts Strong Hiring, Asset Flows in Wealth Division
feed.summary: Wells Fargo's CEO, Charlie Scharf, highlighted strong performance in the company's wealth division, which saw a 29% increase in net income in the fourth quarter. The growth was attributed to increased hiring, declining attrition, and accelerating net asset flows in the latter half of 2025. Despite missing overall profit estimates due to severance costs, the wealth management segment, managing $2.5 trillion in assets, demonstrated significant momentum.

feed.title: Alphabear dev Spry Fox reveals CEOs and staff have taken pay cuts to finish its "biggest game ever" after "extremely fast" spinout from Netflix
feed.summary: Spry Fox, known for Alphabear and Triple Town, has spun out from Netflix after only three months. The founders and staff have taken significant pay cuts and redistributed equity to employees to finance their independence and complete their "biggest title ever," cozy multiplayer life sim Spirit Crossing. Spirit Crossing is planned for a spring beta and a 2026 launch on Steam and other platforms, in addition to being available as a free mobile title for Netflix subscribers.

feed.title: DuPont de Nemours, Inc. (NYSE:DD) Short Interest Update
feed.summary: DuPont de Nemours, Inc. (NYSE:DD) experienced a significant 41.1% drop in short interest in December, bringing the total to 8,980,282 shares. Company insiders, including SVP Erik T. Hoover and CEO Lori Koch, sold shares in November, while institutional investors modified their holdings. Analysts have a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for DD and project 4.38 earnings per share for the current year.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley see double-digit profit jumps amid surging stock market
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley both reported double-digit profit increases in the fourth quarter, driven by a strong stock market and increased deal-making activity. Goldman Sachs's net earnings rose 12% to $4.62 billion, while Morgan Stanley's profit reached $4.4 billion. Both banks also saw significant jumps in investment fee revenues and backlogs, reflecting broader positive trends in the financial industry despite some ongoing tensions between Wall Street and the White House.
